attacking_style: Attacks breakaways on penultimate climbs rather than waiting for finale, exploiting psychological hesitation before biggest ascents. Prefers early destruction of break (15-20km from finish) to solo rides rather than tactical waiting.
team_tactics: Historically elite super-domestique (paced NIBALI, CARAPAZ to 3km), but when given freedom enters breaks early and forces tempo independently. Team support minimal when in GC mode; operates as lone ranger exploiting coordination gaps.
terrain_preference: Medium-mountain summit finishes where diesel strength outlasts explosive climbers. Excels in extreme/crisis conditions. Vulnerable on short, explosive climbs where pure acceleration required. ITT competent enough to gain time on GC climbers.
vulnerability: Unwilling to cooperate in breaks when perceiving unfavorable odds (Kämna standoff). Dropped early on final climbs when in pure domestique role. Limited upside against superior pure climbers (Evenepoel) in direct confrontation.
key_patterns: Attacks penultimate climb in breakaways, not finale: 2+ stage wins using this pattern.
Enters breaks despite close GC position, forcing peloton work through coordination gaps (23sec deficit example).
Late-career transformation from domestique to GC threat at age 33+: Giro podium, Vuelta stage win.
Steals intermediate bonuses and KOM points through tactical awareness independent of primary objective.
Exceptional diesel/recovery strength: bridged solo, finished in GC group after earlier break effort.
Refuses cooperation when perceiving disadvantage; tactical standoffs cost stage opportunities but preserve positioning.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed continued breakaway capacity and diesel strength (Tirreno bridge), but tactical standoffs and inability to contest against top climbers suggest plateau after 2021 peak podium/stage wins.
